source_text: The certificate type MUST be X.509v3 [RFC5280], unless explicitly negotiated otherwise (e.g., [RFC7250]).
rule_statement: The server certificate type MUST be X.509v3 unless another type is explicitly negotiated.
applies_when: Server sends Certificate without negotiating alternative certificate type
checkability_rationale: Can verify certificate format is X.509v3.

source_text: The server's end-entity certificate's public key (and associated restrictions) MUST be compatible with the selected authentication algorithm from the client's "signature_algorithms" extension (currently RSA, ECDSA, or EdDSA).
rule_statement: The server's end-entity certificate public key MUST be compatible with the selected signature algorithm from the client's signature_algorithms extension.
expected_value: public key type matches selected signature algorithm
checkability_rationale: Can verify key type matches advertised signature algorithms.

source_text: The certificate MUST allow the key to be used for signing (i.e., the digitalSignature bit MUST be set if the Key Usage extension is present) with a signature scheme indicated in the client's "signature_algorithms"/"signature_algorithms_cert" extensions (see Section 4.2.3).
rule_statement: The server certificate MUST allow signing (digitalSignature bit set if Key Usage present) with a scheme from the client's signature_algorithms.
applies_when: Server selects certificate for TLS 1.3 authentication
expected_value: digitalSignature bit set (if Key Usage present)
checkability_rationale: Can check Key Usage bits and signature scheme compatibility.

source_text: All certificates provided by the server MUST be signed by a signature algorithm advertised by the client if it is able to provide such a chain (see Section 4.2.3).
rule_statement: All server certificates MUST be signed by a signature algorithm advertised by the client, if such a chain can be provided.
applies_when: Server can produce a chain signed only with client-advertised algorithms
checkability_rationale: Can verify each certificate's signature algorithm against client's advertised list.
ambiguity_notes: Conditional on server being able to produce such a chain; fallback behavior is a SHOULD.

source_text: This fallback chain SHOULD NOT use the deprecated SHA-1 hash algorithm in general, but MAY do so if the client's advertisement permits it, and MUST NOT do so otherwise.
rule_statement: A fallback certificate chain MUST NOT use SHA-1 unless the client's signature_algorithms advertisement permits it.
applies_when: Server sends fallback certificate chain with algorithms not known to be supported by client
expected_value: no SHA-1 unless client advertises it
checkability_rationale: Can verify SHA-1 is not used when client did not advertise it.
extraction_confidence_rationale: Explicit MUST NOT with clear condition.

source_text: If the client cannot construct an acceptable chain using the provided certificates and decides to abort the handshake, then it MUST abort the handshake with an appropriate certificate-related alert (by default, "unsupported_certificate"; see Section 6.2 for more information).
rule_statement: If the client cannot accept the certificate chain and decides to abort, it MUST use a certificate-related alert (default: unsupported_certificate).
applies_when: Client cannot construct acceptable chain from server's Certificate
checkability_rationale: Can verify correct alert is sent on invalid certificate chain.

source_text: As servers MAY require the presence of the "server_name" extension, clients SHOULD send this extension, when applicable.
rule_statement: Clients SHOULD send the server_name extension when applicable.
applies_when: Client connects to a named server
checkability_rationale: Can check presence of server_name in ClientHello.
